article_meta.title: Administration Trump says there will ‘likely be more’ American deaths in Iran before conflict ends by Max Rego - 03\/01\

reasoning: The summary stays neutral, avoids the source’s emotive language, and attributes charged claims to speakers. It does not adopt Trump’s inflammatory phrasing and presents operational details factually. Minor attribution could be clearer on Iran’s interim council, but overall framing remains measured and non-loaded.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses neutral verbs (acknowledged, stated, targeted) and avoids emotive phrasing.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: States Khamenei’s death and the interim council as fact without explicit attribution in the summary, though supported by the article; most other claims are attributed.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: No subjective judgments presented as fact; evaluative statements are attributed to Trump.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: No heightened or alarming language beyond factual reporting of conflict details.
custom_reasoning.mudslinging: No personal attacks in the model’s voice; Trump’s characterization is paraphrased neutrally.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: Does not infer intentions or thoughts of actors.
custom_reasoning.slant: Slight emphasis on U.S. operational perspective consistent with the article; includes Iranian state TV detail.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: All major perspectives present in the article (Trump, Centcom, Press TV, Daily Mail) are included.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: No loaded adjectives used by the model.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Reflects the article’s conflict facts without amplifying negativity.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Gives balanced weight to official sources cited by the article; no undue deference.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Replaces loaded source language with neutral paraphrase and clear attribution.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Captures all distinct sources and perspectives mentioned in the article proportionally.
